description: Fix scaffolder SDK version drift: the scaffolder resolves dependency versions from the locally bundled SDK, which can be stale when the global CLI is outdated. Query the npm registry as the primary source, falling back to local resolution for offline use.

userStories.title: Query npm registry for latest SDK version in scaffolder
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want `opentabs plugin create` to always scaffold with the latest published SDK version so that my new plugin starts with up-to-date dependencies regardless of which CLI version I have installed.
acceptanceCriteria: resolvePluginSdkVersions() queries `npm view @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version` via child_process.execFile with a 5-second timeout as the primary version source
On success, the npm registry version is used for both @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools in the scaffolded package.json
The zod peer dependency version is still read from the local SDK (it tracks SDK API compatibility)
The existing import.meta.resolve logic is extracted into a separate readLocalSdkVersions() function and used as the fallback when npm view fails

The scaffolder logs the resolved version and its source (e.g., 'Using @opentabs-dev packages v0.0.60 (from npm registry)' or 'Using @opentabs-dev packages v0.0.55 (from local CLI — npm registry unreachable)')
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts, function resolvePluginSdkVersions() at lines 64-83.

The current implementation uses import.meta.resolve('@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk') to find the SDK bundled with the running CLI binary and reads its version. When the global CLI (/opt/homebrew/bin/opentabs) is at v0.0.55 but the latest published version is v0.0.60, the scaffolded plugin gets ^0.0.55. Since these are pre-1.0 packages, ^0.0.55 pins to >=0.0.55 <0.0.56, so npm install gives exactly 0.0.55 — not 0.0.60.

Use child_process.execFile('npm', ['view', '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk', 'version'], { timeout: 5000 }) as the primary source. npm view uses the user's ~/.npmrc auth token, which is required since @opentabs-dev packages are private. Direct HTTP fetch to the registry won't work without reimplementing npm's auth resolution.

Extract the current import.meta.resolve logic into readLocalSdkVersions() for the offline fallback. The version source logging should appear in the scaffoldPlugin() function (line ~163) alongside the existing 'Creating opentabs-plugin-...' output.

Key constraint: the scaffolder is also used by platform/create-plugin/src/index.ts which imports scaffoldPlugin from @opentabs-dev/cli/scaffold. The fix must work for both entry points.

Both @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk (dependency) and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools (devDependency) must use the same resolved version string.

userStories.title: Add tests for scaffolder version resolution
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want test coverage for the scaffolder's version resolution logic so that regressions in version drift are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: when npm view succeeds, the scaffolded package.json uses the npm registry version (not the local SDK version)
Test: when npm view fails (offline/auth error/timeout), the scaffolder falls back to the local SDK version without throwing
Test: both @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools receive the same resolved version
Test: the resolved version is a valid semver caret range (matches /^\^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/), never '*'
userStories.notes:
Tests go in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts (create if it doesn't exist). The scaffold function writes files to disk, so tests need a temp directory.

The tricky part is mocking child_process.execFile for the npm view call. Use vi.mock('node:child_process') to mock execFile. For the success case, return '0.0.99\n' on stdout. For the failure case, have the callback receive an Error. For the timeout case, have the mock delay beyond the timeout.

Also check platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts — it already has scaffolding tests. Consider adding an assertion there that the dependency versions are valid semver ranges (not '*').

The existing test infrastructure in the create-plugin package uses a real filesystem and runs the CLI as a subprocess. The new unit tests in scaffold.test.ts should mock the subprocess and filesystem for isolation.
